finding: Correctness/patterns/concurrency/state pass: is_built_in filter correct, all 3 callers pass is_built_in, accessor predicate mirrors list_accessible_mcp_servers, unwrap_or_default fails closed. No defect.

Loopback-SSRF guard keyed on is_built_in, not on the link host being loopback. An admin-registered SYSTEM server (is_system=true, is_built_in=false) registered at http://127.0.0.1:<port> now contributes 127.0.0.1 to every group member's trust set; an injected external server could then emit a resource_link at 127.0.0.1:<any-port> (port-ignored match) and SSRF to loopback services. Under the OLD is_system filter such a server was excluded.
disposition: accepted-tradeoff — consistent with the PRE-EXISTING same-host-trust design, which already trusts loopback hosts of user-registered non-system servers (locked by http_link_matched_trusted_host_is_ingested + the module's 'same-host multi-container' docs). The fix only extends that trust from user-registered to the MORE-privileged admin-registered system servers; registering a system server at loopback is a deliberate admin act; IMDS/link-local stay blocked. Resolution: tighten the misleading comments to state accurately that ONLY built-in loopback hosts are excluded, and record the tradeoff in DECISIONS DEC-3. No logic change (excluding loopback would regress the pre-existing user-loopback case + break the loopback-mock test stand-in).

finding: The accessor widens the default trust set to admin-registered system-server hosts for every group member (previously required ZIEE_MCP_RESOURCE_LINK_ALLOW_PRIVATE). Port-ignored same-host trust means one admin server on a shared internal host lets an injected external link reach any port on that host under MCP_USER (RFC1918 only; IMDS blocked).
disposition: accepted-tradeoff — the documented design intent of this fix (enable rcpa/dscc/biognosia re-hosting without the global env opt-in); port-ignored same-host trust is the pre-existing module design; IMDS stays blocked. Recorded in DEC-2/DEC-5.

finding: .unwrap_or_default() on the accessor silently swallows a DB error with no log — a NEW failure mode on the chat path; a transient DB hiccup makes a legitimate same-host artifact silently fail to ingest with zero diagnostic.
disposition: FIX — extract a shared result_link_trusted_hosts() helper that logs a warn! on Err before degrading to the strict policy.

finding: The trust-host query is per external tool-result (not memoized across a turn) and also runs when resource_links is Some(empty vec). Only fires for external emitters carrying a resource_links field.
disposition: accepted — only runs for external (non-built-in) emitters that returned a resource_links field; one 2-column indexed SELECT is negligible next to the HTTP fetch + ingest that follow. Turn-level memoization is out of scope (over-engineering); the empty-vec case is a rare no-op. Noted, not fixed.

finding: Unit test trusted_hosts_excludes_builtin_loopback_includes_admin_system comment claims to guard 'the regression this fix targets', but trusted_hosts_from_servers only sees ONE bool, so reverting the filter !is_built_in->!is_system passes it identically. The real is_built_in-vs-is_system guard lives in the accessor/integration tests.
disposition: FIX — the accessor integration test accessor_returns_system_host_and_omits_builtin IS the real guard (a system server, is_system=true throughout, is included while is_built_in=false and dropped when is_built_in=true; reverting the filter to !is_system would break its first assertion). Correct the unit test's comment to not overclaim, and rely on the accessor test + the new helper test as the fix-line guards.

finding: The chat-extension glue `if server.is_built_in { Vec::new() } else { accessor(user_id) }` at both call sites is exercised by NO test — a wrong user_id, always-Vec::new(), or inverted branch would be uncaught (integration tests call persist_links + accessor directly, bypassing the glue).
disposition: FIX — extract the branch+query+warn into a shared result_link_trusted_hosts(emitter_is_built_in, user_id) helper called by all 3 sites, and add an integration test asserting built-in emitter -> empty and external emitter -> the registered system host. The live-container repro in Phase 8 additionally exercises the full chat glue end-to-end.

finding: The workflow ToolDispatcher trust-host derivation was migrated to the accessor but has no test; the is_built_in early-return + accessor call in the workflow branch are untested.
disposition: FIX — after the shared-helper extraction the workflow site calls the identical result_link_trusted_hosts() helper as the chat sites, so the helper integration test covers all 3 sites' branch logic.
